+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADDS ALEXANDRA CAMPBELL-DIEHL AS LABOR & EMPLOYMENT ASSOCIATE IN BOSTON

BOSTON - April 22, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has added Alexandra Campbell-Diehl to its Boston office as an associate in the firm's Labor & Employment Practice.

Campbell-Diehl advises employers on matters including single-plaintiff and class action employment litigation related to wrongful termination, harassment, discrimination, retaliation, and more, as well as workplace investigations and corporate transactions. Her work focuses on traditional labor law issues, including employer rights and obligations related to unions, and advising on union organizing, collective bargaining, and labor law developments. She has defended employers in attorney general investigations, state and federal litigation, and proceedings before the National Labor Relations Board, Department of Labor, Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, and the Massachusetts Commission Against Discrimination.

Campbell-Diehl earned her J.D. from Boston College Law School and a B.S. from Cornell University.

"We are pleased to welcome Alex to the Boston Labor & Employment team," Terence P. McCourt and David J. Dykeman, co-managing shareholders of Greenberg Traurig's Boston office, said in a joint statement. "She brings a strong foundation in both employment law and traditional labor law, with experience advising employers on various employment matters in the workplace. Alex's addition enhances our capacity to support clients facing an increasingly dynamic and regulated employment landscape."

+ MORGAN LEWIS ADVISES QUINTILLION ON SALE TO GCI HOLDINGS LLC

PITTSBURGH, April 22, 2026: Morgan Lewis is representing Q Gateway Intermediate Holdings LLC (Quintillion) in connection with a definitive agreement under which GCI Holdings LLC (GCI), a wholly owned subsidiary of GCI Liberty Inc. and Grain Management LLC, will acquire 100% of Quintillion's equity. The transaction will combine Quintillion's more than 1,800 miles of existing subsea and terrestrial fiber and approximately 1,500 miles of planned expansion with GCI's statewide network and operations, further supporting the delivery of reliable, high-speed connectivity across Alaska.

Quintillion is a communications infrastructure provider in Alaska, providing subsea and terrestrial fiber connectivity primarily on a wholesale basis through a total network spanning more than 3,300 route miles of subsea and terrestrial fiber. Headquartered in Alaska, GCI provides data, mobile, voice, and managed services to consumer, business, government, and carrier customers throughout the state, serving more than 200 communities.

+ MORGAN LEWIS ANNOUNCES MICHAEL RINEHART AS CHIEF INFORMATION OFFICER

PHILADELPHIA, April 22, 2026: Morgan Lewis is pleased to announce that Michael Rinehart has joined the firm as chief information officer, strengthening the firm's technology platform and cybersecurity capabilities. Michael joins from Dechert, where he served as CIO, overseeing the growth and implementation of the firm's information technology strategy.

In his role, Michael will lead the firm's global information technology function, focusing on the continued development of secure, reliable, and scalable systems that support the firm's lawyers and the delivery of client service. He brings significant experience in cybersecurity and enterprise infrastructure, further reinforcing the firm's approach to information security and risk management. Michael will work in close coordination with Chief AI & Knowledge Management Officer Colleen Nihill and her team to ensure alignment between the firm's technology platform and its data, AI, and knowledge initiatives.

Michael brings more than 25 years of experience across professional services and industry and focuses on information technology strategy, infrastructure, and operations, including enterprise systems, cybersecurity, and digital transformation. Prior to Dechert, Michael served as chief information officer at Fox Rothschild, where he led technology strategy and operations for a decade. Earlier in his career, he held a range of technology leadership roles in professional services and industry.

"Technology is central to how we operate and deliver value to our clients," said Tony Licata, chief operating officer of Morgan Lewis. "Michael brings a strong track record of leading enterprise technology functions in complex professional services organizations, with particular depth in infrastructure and cybersecurity. His collaborative approach will help ensure our lawyers have the systems and support they need, while we continue to advance our technology priorities in coordination with our AI and knowledge management initiatives."

+ MORGAN LEWIS REPRESENTS LIME ROCK NEW ENERGY ON CLOSING $640 MILLION FUND II

NEW YORK, April 22, 2026: Morgan Lewis advised Lime Rock New Energy in the closing of Lime Rock New Energy Fund II (Fund II) with total commitments of $640 million. The fund closed well above its $500 million target and just below its $650 million hard cap, representing a doubling of committed capital relative to its predecessor fund.

Lime Rock New Energy is a growth-focused private equity partner for entrepreneurs and management teams building differentiated, innovative businesses across the energy transition. Fund II attracted commitments from a range of institutional investors, including asset managers, university endowments, and climate-focused family offices, with approximately one-third of commitments coming from European and Asian institutions.

The Morgan Lewis team that advised Lime Rock New Energy included partners Daniel A. Losk, Richard S. Zarin, Steven Lightstone, and Christine Ayako Schleppegrell and associates Joe Koronkowski, Thomas J. Crociata, and Yara Ismael.

DASHBOARD: FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP

PermID4297411712Websitehttp://www.foxrothschild.com/IndustrySpecialized Maritime-shipping law Firm

ACTIVITIES:

Fox, Rothschild, O'Brien & Frankel, LLP provides legal services to businesses and individual clients throughout the greater Delaware Valley and central and southern New Jersey, as well as across the United States. The firms area of practice includes business, corporate and securities, labor and employment, real estate, creditors' rights, litigation, and tax and estates law. The firm was founded in 1907 and is based Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. It has offices in Doylestown, Exton, and Lansdale, Pennsylvania; Atlantic City, and Princeton, New Jersey; and Wilmington, Delaware.

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP [6 Press Releases]

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP CATHERINE MITCHELL AND THOMAS TERRELL SELECTED TO NORTH CAROLINA LAWYERS WEEKLY'S REAL ESTATE POWER LIST

Fox Rothschild congratulates Catherine B. Mitchell and Thomas E. Terrell Jr. who were named to North Carolina Lawyers Weekly's 2025 Real Estate Power List, which highlights North Carolina's leading real estate attorneys.

Mitchell, selected to the list for the second consecutive year, has handled more than $2 billion in transactions throughout her 14-year career. Mitchell is noted by North Carolina Lawyers Weekly as a "skilled advocate for national and regional clients in commercial real estate transactions throughout the development process." She advises clients on property tax exemptions for low-income housing and handles all aspects of commercial real estate transactions, including acquisitions, dispositions, leasing and financing. Her clients range from landlords and tenants to health care providers, developers and life insurance companies.

Mitchell is a Fellow of the American College of Mortgage Attorneys and a member of the University of North Carolina Asheville Board of Trustees. She also provides pro bono representation to businesses in Asheville that were affected by flooding from Hurricane Helene.

With four decades of practice, Terrell has handled zoning and land use matters in 88 of North Carolina's 100 counties, and in over 450 local governments across six states. He is hired in "hard-to-permit" industries, including mining, landfills and renewable energy.

Fox Rothschild congratulates Heidy Vaquerano for being named to Los Angeles Business Journal's Leaders of Influence: Thriving in Their 40s list.

Vaquerano is among 40 individuals recognized for their skills and ability to "help set growth in motion for businesses in virtually any industry."

A partner in the firm's Entertainment & Sports Law Department, Vaquerano represents musicians, producers, actors, writers, production companies, record labels, tech startups, private-equity funds and other entertainment industry players.

She possesses a deep understanding of the evolving laws and technology impacting the music industry. Vaquerano works with artists and rights companies at the forefront of a major shift in the industry to help capitalize on market opportunities.

Notably, she has represented intellectual property-focused investment firm HarbourView Equity Partners since its founding in 2021. "In the past calendar year, she was instrumental in the client securing a combined $3 billion in debt financing through securitization for catalogues," the Los Angeles Business Journal reported. + FOX ROTHSCHILD DONATES $1 MILLION TO SUPPORT YOUTH PROGRAMS THROUGH GENERATION FOX INITIATIVE

Fox Rothschild's Generation Fox community service initiative has donated $1 million, more than 5,000 items and 800+ service hours to more than 225 organizations that empower youth from coast to coast.

"Giving back has always been deeply personal to us and our firm culture - it's part of who we are," said Firmwide Managing Partner Todd A. Rodriguez. "With Generation Fox, we've put youth at the heart of our charitable efforts and community engagement. Our goal is to make a difference, and the results speak for themselves."

Launched in July 2024, Generation Fox invests in and supports youth in the communities where the firm has offices across the U.S. The initiative encompasses a multitude of ways that Fox gives back to its communities, with a focus on children up to age 18.

During the holidays, Generation Fox is stepping up its commitment to supporting local schools by providing essential supplies and financial assistance. Our offices have organized events or donation drives addressing the most urgent needs of schools in their communities.

"While every one of our offices has its own unique character, we are united by a shared purpose: to empower youth in our communities," said Holly Lentz Kleeman, Chief Business Development and Marketing Officer at Fox. "By collaborating with our local schools we are making a shared investment in the future."

Generation Fox was designed to be flexible so that lawyers, practice groups, offices and the firm's business professionals can each work and donate in ways that make sense in their cities and neighborhoods. The goals are equally expansive: to support groups that empower kids in their education and in their play, in cultural development, in nutrition, in health and well-being, and through protection of their legal rights.

Part of the publication's Women of Influence program, Richmond's selection reflects her significant and lasting impact on the hospitality sector and her commitment to advancing workplace compliance and innovation.

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP RICK COUGHLIN, JEFF MACHARG AND BRAD RISINGER SELECTED AS MANAGING PARTNERS TO WATCH

Fox Rothschild congratulates Greensboro, Charlotte and Raleigh Office Managing Partners, Richard A. Coughlin, Jeffrey P. MacHarg and Bradley M. Risinger, who were all recognized by North Carolina Lawyers Weekly as Managing Partners to Watch.

The annual feature spotlights managing partners who are "equal parts administrative genius and skilled legal practitioner."

Coughlin, selected for the second consecutive year, became Greensboro Managing Partner in 2021. Under his leadership the office has grown to become firm's largest in the Carolinas. Coughlin also serves as the firm's Deputy General Counsel where he plays an important part in helping lawyers navigate difficult situations. He told North Carolina Lawyers Weekly that, "if you put in enough effort and promote good communication, you usually can find a solution that benefits everyone." With an eye toward giving back to the community, Coughlin worked with the office to pledge $100,000 to support a Cone Health mobile health unit that will provide essential medical services to underserved communities in North Carolina.

Honoring his commitment to leadership and adaptability, this marks MacHarg's third year on the list. Since assuming the Charlotte Managing Partner role in 2021, he has doubled the number of attorneys in the office. MacHarg also led the team's relocation effort, moving the office into the heart of Charlotte's central business district. He maintains an active litigation practice with clients spanning a variety of industries. Colleagues continue to be inspired by the passion he brings to both roles, noting to North Carolina Lawyers Weekly that MacHarg remains "fully engaged and has made a significant impact on office life."

Risinger took the reins as Raleigh Managing Partner in 2022. He brings 35 years of legal experience to the role. A seasoned trial lawyer whose practice centers on commercial disputes, he is highlighted for representing law firms and media outlets denied access to public records by law enforcement - all cases that raise concerns about First Amendment rights and government transparency. A zealous advocate, Risinger says that "especially in today's climate, this is a fight that's always worth waging." North Carolina Lawyers Weekly also cited his involvement in local community organizations, including United Way of North Carolina and NC 211, the state's health and human services referral network.

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

(January 6, 2026, Los Angeles) - Steptoe LLP associate Conor Tucker has been appointed as Chair of the Beverly Hills Bar Association's Appellate Law Section for the 2025 - 2026 bar year.

Conor is an experienced litigator who has represented clients at every level of the federal court system - including serving as Counsel of Record before the United States Supreme Court. He has guided cases from the earliest stages of investigation through trial and appeal and has argued key motions and appeals in both state and federal courts. Conor's advocacy - in briefing and at argument- has led to published, precedent-setting decisions, including issues of first impression. urn:contentItem:6JGG-8H13-RRXY-F1J8-00000-00FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS: LEADERS & PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:00:48ZLexisNexisSTOCKFOX ROTHSCHILD LLP - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS: LEADERS & PEERS

DASHBOARD: FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP

PermID4297411712Websitehttp://www.foxrothschild.com/IndustrySpecialized Maritime-shipping law Firm

ACTIVITIES:

Fox, Rothschild, O'Brien & Frankel, LLP provides legal services to businesses and individual clients throughout the greater Delaware Valley and central and southern New Jersey, as well as across the United States. The firms area of practice includes business, corporate and securities, labor and employment, real estate, creditors' rights, litigation, and tax and estates law. The firm was founded in 1907 and is based Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. It has offices in Doylestown, Exton, and Lansdale, Pennsylvania; Atlantic City, and Princeton, New Jersey; and Wilmington, Delaware.

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP [6 Press Releases]

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP CATHERINE MITCHELL AND THOMAS TERRELL SELECTED TO NORTH CAROLINA LAWYERS WEEKLY'S REAL ESTATE POWER LIST

Fox Rothschild congratulates Catherine B. Mitchell and Thomas E. Terrell Jr. who were named to North Carolina Lawyers Weekly's 2025 Real Estate Power List, which highlights North Carolina's leading real estate attorneys.

Mitchell, selected to the list for the second consecutive year, has handled more than $2 billion in transactions throughout her 14-year career. Mitchell is noted by North Carolina Lawyers Weekly as a "skilled advocate for national and regional clients in commercial real estate transactions throughout the development process." She advises clients on property tax exemptions for low-income housing and handles all aspects of commercial real estate transactions, including acquisitions, dispositions, leasing and financing. Her clients range from landlords and tenants to health care providers, developers and life insurance companies.

Mitchell is a Fellow of the American College of Mortgage Attorneys and a member of the University of North Carolina Asheville Board of Trustees. She also provides pro bono representation to businesses in Asheville that were affected by flooding from Hurricane Helene.

With four decades of practice, Terrell has handled zoning and land use matters in 88 of North Carolina's 100 counties, and in over 450 local governments across six states. He is hired in "hard-to-permit" industries, including mining, landfills and renewable energy.

Source: Company Website

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP HEIDY VAQUERANO NAMED TO LA BUSINESS JOURNAL'S LEADERS OF INFLUENCE LIST

Fox Rothschild congratulates Heidy Vaquerano for being named to Los Angeles Business Journal's Leaders of Influence: Thriving in Their 40s list.

Vaquerano is among 40 individuals recognized for their skills and ability to "help set growth in motion for businesses in virtually any industry."

A partner in the firm's Entertainment & Sports Law Department, Vaquerano represents musicians, producers, actors, writers, production companies, record labels, tech startups, private-equity funds and other entertainment industry players.

She possesses a deep understanding of the evolving laws and technology impacting the music industry. Vaquerano works with artists and rights companies at the forefront of a major shift in the industry to help capitalize on market opportunities.

Notably, she has represented intellectual property-focused investment firm HarbourView Equity Partners since its founding in 2021. "In the past calendar year, she was instrumental in the client securing a combined $3 billion in debt financing through securitization for catalogues," the Los Angeles Business Journal reported. The debt financings were supported by more than 75 music catalogs from a variety of artists whose acquisitions were negotiated by Vaquerano and the Fox team.

Source: Company Website

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD DONATES $1 MILLION TO SUPPORT YOUTH PROGRAMS THROUGH GENERATION FOX INITIATIVE

Fox Rothschild's Generation Fox community service initiative has donated $1 million, more than 5,000 items and 800+ service hours to more than 225 organizations that empower youth from coast to coast.

"Giving back has always been deeply personal to us and our firm culture - it's part of who we are," said Firmwide Managing Partner Todd A. Rodriguez. "With Generation Fox, we've put youth at the heart of our charitable efforts and community engagement. Our goal is to make a difference, and the results speak for themselves."

Launched in July 2024, Generation Fox invests in and supports youth in the communities where the firm has offices across the U.S. The initiative encompasses a multitude of ways that Fox gives back to its communities, with a focus on children up to age 18.

During the holidays, Generation Fox is stepping up its commitment to supporting local schools by providing essential supplies and financial assistance. Our offices have organized events or donation drives addressing the most urgent needs of schools in their communities.

"While every one of our offices has its own unique character, we are united by a shared purpose: to empower youth in our communities," said Holly Lentz Kleeman, Chief Business Development and Marketing Officer at Fox. "By collaborating with our local schools we are making a shared investment in the future."

Generation Fox was designed to be flexible so that lawyers, practice groups, offices and the firm's business professionals can each work and donate in ways that make sense in their cities and neighborhoods. The goals are equally expansive: to support groups that empower kids in their education and in their play, in cultural development, in nutrition, in health and well-being, and through protection of their legal rights.

Source: Company Website

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP CAROLYN RICHMOND HONORED BY NEW YORK BUSINESS JOURNAL

Fox Rothschild congratulates Carolyn Richmond, who was recognized by the New York Business Journal as one of ten honorees who "exemplify influence in both their professional and their personal endeavors."

Part of the publication's Women of Influence program, Richmond's selection reflects her significant and lasting impact on the hospitality sector and her commitment to advancing workplace compliance and innovation.

Chair of the firm's Hospitality Practice, Richmond is a trusted employment attorney to New York's restaurant industry. She helps clients address evolving challenges, including wage and hour laws, workplace regulations, union organizing and crisis management.

Source: Company Website

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP RICK COUGHLIN, JEFF MACHARG AND BRAD RISINGER SELECTED AS MANAGING PARTNERS TO WATCH

Fox Rothschild congratulates Greensboro, Charlotte and Raleigh Office Managing Partners, Richard A. Coughlin, Jeffrey P. MacHarg and Bradley M. Risinger, who were all recognized by North Carolina Lawyers Weekly as Managing Partners to Watch.

The annual feature spotlights managing partners who are "equal parts administrative genius and skilled legal practitioner."

Coughlin, selected for the second consecutive year, became Greensboro Managing Partner in 2021. Under his leadership the office has grown to become firm's largest in the Carolinas. Coughlin also serves as the firm's Deputy General Counsel where he plays an important part in helping lawyers navigate difficult situations. He told North Carolina Lawyers Weekly that, "if you put in enough effort and promote good communication, you usually can find a solution that benefits everyone." With an eye toward giving back to the community, Coughlin worked with the office to pledge $100,000 to support a Cone Health mobile health unit that will provide essential medical services to underserved communities in North Carolina.

Honoring his commitment to leadership and adaptability, this marks MacHarg's third year on the list. Since assuming the Charlotte Managing Partner role in 2021, he has doubled the number of attorneys in the office. MacHarg also led the team's relocation effort, moving the office into the heart of Charlotte's central business district. He maintains an active litigation practice with clients spanning a variety of industries. Colleagues continue to be inspired by the passion he brings to both roles, noting to North Carolina Lawyers Weekly that MacHarg remains "fully engaged and has made a significant impact on office life."

Risinger took the reins as Raleigh Managing Partner in 2022. He brings 35 years of legal experience to the role. A seasoned trial lawyer whose practice centers on commercial disputes, he is highlighted for representing law firms and media outlets denied access to public records by law enforcement - all cases that raise concerns about First Amendment rights and government transparency. A zealous advocate, Risinger says that "especially in today's climate, this is a fight that's always worth waging." North Carolina Lawyers Weekly also cited his involvement in local community organizations, including United Way of North Carolina and NC 211, the state's health and human services referral network.

Source: Company Website

+ GAMING PARTNER KIERON FRAZIER JOINS FOX ROTHSCHILD IN CHICAGO

Fox Rothschild welcomes Kieron A. Frazier to the Chicago office as a Partner in the Gaming Group.

"With Illinois' gaming industry continuing to boom, we are thrilled to welcome Kieron to our Chicago office," said Gordon E. Gouveia, Fox's Chicago Office Managing Partner. "Kieron's depth of experience in the gaming sector, combined with his unique background in sports law make him a valuable addition to our respected gaming industry team."

A corporate and transactional attorney, Frazier focuses his practice on the gaming industry.

He provides advice on regulatory compliance, licensure and internal investigations in addition to transactions and other general corporate matters. Frazier's clients include leaders in sports wagering, fantasy sports, iGaming, horse tracks, video gaming and lotteries. He also supports tribal licensees and social gaming operators and investors.

In addition, Frazier counsels clients on a variety of corporate and finance matters, including team-level transactional work, financing, media transactions, mergers and acquisitions.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

+ JONES DAY [3 Press Releases]

+ FORMER SEC DEPUTY DIRECTOR NEKIA HACKWORTH JOINS JONES DAY AS PARTNER IN ATLANTA

Nekia Hackworth has joined Jones Day as a partner in its Investigations & White Collar Defense Practice. She is based in the Firm's Atlanta Office.

Ms. Hackworth has more than 20 years of experience in both government and private practice as a lawyer representing clients in white-collar criminal cases and civil litigation, conducting government and internal investigations, and counseling companies on corporate compliance matters. She served most recently as the Deputy Director of Enforcement in the Southeast Region for the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), where she supervised more than 250 professionals with responsibility for SEC matters across three offices (Washington, Atlanta, and Miami) and 11 jurisdictions. She oversaw hundreds of investigations in her tenure at the SEC, including suspected market manipulation, insider trading, and investment adviser fraud.

Source: Company Website

+ FORMER SEC DEPUTY DIRECTOR NEKIA HACKWORTH JOINS JONES DAY AS PARTNER IN ATLANTA

Nekia Hackworth has joined Jones Day as a partner in its Investigations & White Collar Defense Practice. She is based in the Firm's Atlanta Office.

Ms. Hackworth has more than 20 years of experience in both government and private practice as a lawyer representing clients in white-collar criminal cases and civil litigation, conducting government and internal investigations, and counseling companies on corporate compliance matters. She served most recently as the Deputy Director of Enforcement in the Southeast Region for the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), where she supervised more than 250 professionals with responsibility for SEC matters across three offices (Washington, Atlanta, and Miami) and 11 jurisdictions. She oversaw hundreds of investigations in her tenure at the SEC, including suspected market manipulation, insider trading, and investment adviser fraud.

+ DERIVATIVES LAWYER COLIN RICE JOINS JONES DAY IN SINGAPORE

Colin Rice has joined Jones Day as a partner in its Financial Markets Practice. He is based in the Firm's Singapore Office.

Mr. Rice is an experienced derivatives lawyer who has counseled clients on a broad range of financial markets transactions, with a particular focus on OTC derivatives, structured products, structured finance transactions, repos, and securities lending. He works with global investment banks, retail banks, funds, corporates, and service providers. He also advises on uncleared derivatives margin reform and collateral arrangements, default management, and the impact of global regulatory developments on derivatives businesses and documentation.

"Colin is an important addition to our global derivatives team. Asia is a fast-growing market with more and more netting friendly jurisdictions, and he is among the most experienced lawyers in the region on these matters," said Alban Caillemer du Ferrage, chair of Jones Day's Financial Markets department for EMEA and APAC. "Colin served as a director in the Singapore office of one of the world's largest investment banks. We will be able to work with our largest clients on their most complex derivatives transactions there. I am delighted to welcome him to our global group."

Source: Company Website

+ STEPTOE LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ STEPTOE & JOHNSON LLP CONOR TUCKER APPOINTED CHAIR OF THE BEVERLY HILLS BAR ASSOCIATION'S APPELLATE LAW SECTION

(January 6, 2026, Los Angeles) - Steptoe LLP associate Conor Tucker has been appointed as Chair of the Beverly Hills Bar Association's Appellate Law Section for the 2025 - 2026 bar year.

Conor is an experienced litigator who has represented clients at every level of the federal court system - including serving as Counsel of Record before the United States Supreme Court. He has guided cases from the earliest stages of investigation through trial and appeal and has argued key motions and appeals in both state and federal courts. Conor's advocacy - in briefing and at argument- has led to published, precedent-setting decisions, including issues of first impression. In the California Court of Appeal alone, this includes litigating issues of first impression regarding the impact of COIVD-19 on force majeure clauses in live music performance contracts and state regulation of video streaming services. In the United States Supreme Court, he routinely serves as Counsel of Record for Members of Congress as amici.

He has handled a wide variety of matters, from contract disputes to intellectual property cases involving trademarks, trade secrets, copywrights, and patents, as well as constitutional, criminal procedure, and due process issues. His clients span a broad range of industries, including technology, energy, real estate, retail, apparel, mining, entertainment, banking, healthcare, social media, and computer sciences.

Source: Company Website

+ STEPTOE NAMED TO LEXOLOGY 100: DATA 2026

(December 22, 2025, Washington, DC) - Steptoe has been recognized in the 2026 edition of Lexology 100: Data, Lexology's annual guide profiling the world's leading data law firms. The Lexology 100: Data, formerly known as the GDR 100, is the only global ranking that evaluates firms' capabilities, track records, and market reputations across the full spectrum of data law, including privacy and data protection, AI regulation, online content moderation, cybersecurity, B2B data disputes, and the use of IP and confidentiality frameworks to safeguard proprietary information.

Lexology notes that the 150 firms recognized this year "fully merit the accolade," following a rigorous assessment process focused on firms' depth of practice, major matters, and sustained representation of companies with complex global data needs. The ranking reflects not only personal data work, but also the rapidly growing field of non‑personal data regulation, including AI‑related mandates, online harms laws, and cybersecurity frameworks such as DORA and NIS2.

Source: Company Website

+ STEPTOE & JOHNSON LLP THE HILL NAMES LESLIE BELCHER, ELIZABETH BURKS, AND ROWAN BOST TO TOP LOBBYISTS 2025

(December 12, 2025, Washington, DC) - Leslie Belcher, Elizabeth Burks, and Rowan Bost have been named to The Hill's Top Lobbyists 2025 list. Selected from more than 1,800 nominations the lobbyists on this list represent the industry's savviest, most influential and well-connected advocates across Capitol Hill. The honorees span veterans and up-and-comers, retired military officials and academics, former lawmakers and staffers, and are intended to reflect the wide ranges of experiences and backgrounds of the city's most influential K Street forces.

As Managing Director, Government Affairs & Public Policy at Steptoe, Leslie Belcher, an Oklahoma native, is a power-player and recognized trusted advisor to members of Congress. She offers clients more than 36 years of public and private sector experience, directing government relations campaigns for a variety of clients. Leslie has her finger on the pulse of the House Republican Conference and possesses an extensive knowledge of the legislative process. Her ability to navigate the complex and narrow majority of the GOP Conference, understand intricate policy details, and master the "art of the possible" is unmatched in Washington. Previously, Leslie served as Chief of Staff for Reps. Tom Cole (R-OK) (House Appropriations Chair) and Wes Watkins (R-OK), Legislative Director for Rep. Bill Brewster (D-OK), and Appropriations Associate for Rep. Wes Watkins (D-OK). This bipartisan experience provides Leslie with a unique and valuable perspective.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADDS ALEXANDRA CAMPBELL-DIEHL AS LABOR & EMPLOYMENT ASSOCIATE IN BOSTON

BOSTON - April 22, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has added Alexandra Campbell-Diehl to its Boston office as an associate in the firm's Labor & Employment Practice.

Campbell-Diehl advises employers on matters including single-plaintiff and class action employment litigation related to wrongful termination, harassment, discrimination, retaliation, and more, as well as workplace investigations and corporate transactions. Her work focuses on traditional labor law issues, including employer rights and obligations related to unions, and advising on union organizing, collective bargaining, and labor law developments. She has defended employers in attorney general investigations, state and federal litigation, and proceedings before the National Labor Relations Board, Department of Labor, Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, and the Massachusetts Commission Against Discrimination.

Campbell-Diehl earned her J.D. from Boston College Law School and a B.S. from Cornell University.

"We are pleased to welcome Alex to the Boston Labor & Employment team," Terence P. McCourt and David J. Dykeman, co-managing shareholders of Greenberg Traurig's Boston office, said in a joint statement. "She brings a strong foundation in both employment law and traditional labor law, with experience advising employers on various employment matters in the workplace. Alex's addition enhances our capacity to support clients facing an increasingly dynamic and regulated employment landscape."

Source: Company Website

+ MORGAN LEWIS & BOCKIUS LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ MORGAN LEWIS ADVISES QUINTILLION ON SALE TO GCI HOLDINGS LLC

PITTSBURGH, April 22, 2026: Morgan Lewis is representing Q Gateway Intermediate Holdings LLC (Quintillion) in connection with a definitive agreement under which GCI Holdings LLC (GCI), a wholly owned subsidiary of GCI Liberty Inc. and Grain Management LLC, will acquire 100% of Quintillion's equity. The transaction will combine Quintillion's more than 1,800 miles of existing subsea and terrestrial fiber and approximately 1,500 miles of planned expansion with GCI's statewide network and operations, further supporting the delivery of reliable, high-speed connectivity across Alaska.

Quintillion is a communications infrastructure provider in Alaska, providing subsea and terrestrial fiber connectivity primarily on a wholesale basis through a total network spanning more than 3,300 route miles of subsea and terrestrial fiber. Headquartered in Alaska, GCI provides data, mobile, voice, and managed services to consumer, business, government, and carrier customers throughout the state, serving more than 200 communities.

Morgan Lewis partners Kim Taylor, Greer Longer, Harry Robins, Paul Gordon, and Ulises Pin and associates Matt Cubin, Frank Ren, and Andreas Andrews are advising Quintillion.

Source: Company Website

+ MORGAN LEWIS ANNOUNCES MICHAEL RINEHART AS CHIEF INFORMATION OFFICER

PHILADELPHIA, April 22, 2026: Morgan Lewis is pleased to announce that Michael Rinehart has joined the firm as chief information officer, strengthening the firm's technology platform and cybersecurity capabilities. Michael joins from Dechert, where he served as CIO, overseeing the growth and implementation of the firm's information technology strategy.

In his role, Michael will lead the firm's global information technology function, focusing on the continued development of secure, reliable, and scalable systems that support the firm's lawyers and the delivery of client service. He brings significant experience in cybersecurity and enterprise infrastructure, further reinforcing the firm's approach to information security and risk management. Michael will work in close coordination with Chief AI & Knowledge Management Officer Colleen Nihill and her team to ensure alignment between the firm's technology platform and its data, AI, and knowledge initiatives.

Michael brings more than 25 years of experience across professional services and industry and focuses on information technology strategy, infrastructure, and operations, including enterprise systems, cybersecurity, and digital transformation. Prior to Dechert, Michael served as chief information officer at Fox Rothschild, where he led technology strategy and operations for a decade. Earlier in his career, he held a range of technology leadership roles in professional services and industry.

"Technology is central to how we operate and deliver value to our clients," said Tony Licata, chief operating officer of Morgan Lewis. "Michael brings a strong track record of leading enterprise technology functions in complex professional services organizations, with particular depth in infrastructure and cybersecurity. His collaborative approach will help ensure our lawyers have the systems and support they need, while we continue to advance our technology priorities in coordination with our AI and knowledge management initiatives."

Source: Company Website

+ MORGAN LEWIS REPRESENTS LIME ROCK NEW ENERGY ON CLOSING $640 MILLION FUND II

NEW YORK, April 22, 2026: Morgan Lewis advised Lime Rock New Energy in the closing of Lime Rock New Energy Fund II (Fund II) with total commitments of $640 million. The fund closed well above its $500 million target and just below its $650 million hard cap, representing a doubling of committed capital relative to its predecessor fund.

Lime Rock New Energy is a growth-focused private equity partner for entrepreneurs and management teams building differentiated, innovative businesses across the energy transition. Fund II attracted commitments from a range of institutional investors, including asset managers, university endowments, and climate-focused family offices, with approximately one-third of commitments coming from European and Asian institutions.

The Morgan Lewis team that advised Lime Rock New Energy included partners Daniel A. Losk, Richard S. Zarin, Steven Lightstone, and Christine Ayako Schleppegrell and associates Joe Koronkowski, Thomas J. Crociata, and Yara Ismael.

Source: Company Website
